title: marshallia dentata
intro: Marshallia dentata, also known as the marsh marigold or green-headed coneflower, is a perennial plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Marshallia dentata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestive health, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for convenient and accessible use of its medicinal properties.
